Data Sync & Transfer

×

EXPORT Download Your Data

Export all your data to transfer to another device or for backup.

Complete Backup includes:
  • All asset records
  • Audit trail history
  • Custom departments
  • Warranty overrides

IMPORT Load Data from Backup

Import data from a backup file or cloud storage link.

Supports: Google Drive (share link), Dropbox (direct link), OneDrive (direct link), or any direct JSON file URL.

SHARE Access Data from Any Device

Follow these steps to access your data from any device:

Google Drive
  1. Export your data using "Complete Backup"
  2. Upload the JSON file to Google Drive
  3. Right-click the file → Share → Copy link
  4. On another device, use "Import from URL" and paste the link
Dropbox
  1. Export your data using "Complete Backup"
  2. Upload the JSON file to Dropbox
  3. Click Share → Copy link (change ?dl=0 to ?dl=1 for direct download)
  4. On another device, use "Import from URL" and paste the link
Email or USB Drive
  1. Export your data using "Complete Backup"
  2. Email the JSON file to yourself or copy to USB
  3. On another device, download the file
  4. Use "Import from File" to load the data

or click to browse • Supports .xlsx and .xls files

The system has auto-detected column mappings. Review and adjust if needed.

⚙️ Import Options
👁️ Import Preview
0 Valid 0 Invalid 0 Duplicates
✅
Import Successful!

📋 Required Fields Reference

The following fields are expected. The system will try to match column headers automatically.

1. Asset Code *
Unique identifier (e.g., BRH-MS-001-2025)
2. Asset Description *
Name/description of the asset
3. Date In *
Purchase/acquisition date
4. Supplier
Vendor name or donor
5. Location/Department *
Where the asset is located
6. Source *
Purchased or Donated
7. Month
APR, MAY, JUN, etc. (fiscal year)
8. Category *
Medical Equipment or Furniture & Appliances
9. Serial/Model
Serial number or model number
10. Quantity
Number of items (default: 1)
11. Unit Price
Price per unit (JMD)
12. Total Price
Total value (Qty × Unit Price)

* Required fields • Fields with orange border are optional

📋 Supported Excel Column Headers

The system automatically detects these column headers in your Excel files:

Asset Code *
asset code, code, asset id, asset no, asset number
Description *
description, asset description, item, name
Date In *
date in, purchase date, acquisition date
Department/Location *
department, location, ward, unit, area
Source *
source, acquisition type, funded by
Category *
category, asset category, asset type, class
Supplier/Donor
supplier, vendor, donor
Serial/Model
serial, model, serial number, model number
Quantity & Pricing
quantity, qty, unit price, total price, total

* Required fields • Fields with orange border are optional

Hospital Asset Cost Catalog

Comprehensive Price Reference — Black River Hospital Fixed Asset Register

☁️ Google Drive Sync

Last sync: Never

Last saved by: --

Asset codes from ID Register 1997 & 1998
💡
Comprehensive Hospital Asset Cost Reference Catalog
All prices are in JMD (Jamaican Dollars) and reflect actual recorded purchase data from Black River Hospital's Fixed Asset Register (2022–2025). Items marked ★ have confirmed supplier pricing. Prices include 15% GCT unless noted.
Total Categories
0
Total Items
0
Confirmed Prices (★)
0
Search Results
--

Useful Life & Depreciation Reference

Useful life estimates per category are based on AHA Estimated Useful Lives of Depreciable Hospital Assets, IRS MACRS recovery periods, and industry averages. Depreciation method: Straight Line (SL). Salvage value calculated automatically by asset category percentage (5–15%).

⚠ Price Correction - Overpriced Assets Detected

Asset Code Description Current Price Catalog Match Catalog Price Ratio

Asset Code Parser

Split Multi-Code Assets into Individual Rows

☁️ Google Drive Sync

Last sync: Never

Last saved by: --

Multi-Code Assets Found
0
Individual Rows After Split
0
Total Assets Scanned
0
Selected for Split
0
ⓘ How This Works
This tool detects assets that have multiple asset codes and/or multiple departments packed into a single row (separated by commas). It splits them into individual rows, each with its own asset code and matching department, while keeping all other fields (description, unit price, supplier, etc.) the same. Select the assets you want to split and click "Apply Selected Splits" to create the new rows and remove the original combined row.
🔎
Click "Scan for Multi-Code Assets" to begin
This will search your assets for rows containing multiple asset codes or departments that need splitting.
